name: Thai Fried Noodles (1)
recipeInstructions:
1. If using dried rice noodles, soak in hot water for 20 minutes before cooking. Drain.
2. In 4 quarts boiling water, cook fresh rice noodles 2 to 3 minutes or until just tender to the bite, al dente. Drain.
3. Rinse the noodles and drain again for 30 minutes or until dry.
4. In boiling water, blanch the bean sprouts for 30 seconds. Refresh under cold water. Drain.
5. Heat oil in a large skillet. Fry garlic and shallots until golden. Add shrimp paste and chopped dried shrimp. Stir.
6. Add the shrimp and stir-fry until done. Add fish sauce, vinegar, sugar, ketchup, and chili powder. Stir until sugar dissolves.
7. Add the beaten eggs to the pan and let them set slightly. Then stir to scramble.
8. Add the noodles and toss for about 2 minutes.
9. Place the Pad Thai on a platter. Sprinkle with peanuts, chili flakes, green onions, and coriander. Arrange lime wedges around the edge of the platter.
10. Serve with a side dish of fresh bean sprouts and cucumbers.
(Yield: 3-4 servings)
